profile.name: Riley Glusker
profile.headline: Staff Frontend Engineer at Airbnb (Messaging)
profile.summary: Staff frontend engineer specializing in messaging products, server-driven UI platforms, streaming interfaces, and AI trust patterns. At Airbnb since 2019; previously Goldman Sachs.
profile.tagline: I build streaming interfaces and server-driven UI at Airbnb — and I watch pretty much every horror movie that comes out.

photos.alt: Riley smiling in a lush garden with a palace and pink flowers behind her
Riley smiling at an outdoor café with a glass of wine

experience.title: Staff Software Engineer
experience.notes: Promoted to Senior in 2021, Staff in 2026. Interim EM twice: team of 8 (backend + web), 3-month stretches, while continuing to deliver as an IC.

projects.name: Messaging Cards platform
projects.description: Airbnb's composable cross-platform card platform (web, iOS, Android) — a server-driven UI system that became the default integration path for teams building in Messaging. Patent pending. Includes a self-serve sandbox and card-builder tooling so partner teams can ship without frontend engineers.
highlights: Adopted by many products across a dozen-plus partner teams
Serves hundreds of thousands of cards per day
A backend-only team's first card drove a double-digit lift in reservation alterations and a meaningful drop in support contacts

metrics.label: products and partner teams on the platform
lift in reservation alterations from a backend-only team's first card

projects.name: "Ask anything" listing-page assistant
projects.role: Product surface owner and engineering lead
projects.description: AI assistant on Airbnb listing pages. Owned the full product surface: UI, intent-recognition display, and trust-preserving error states (no ML modeling work). Demoed live to executive leadership.
highlights: Introduced trust-preserving error states distinguishing 'no data' from 'no understanding'

projects.name: Streaming response UX over WebSockets
projects.description: Riley built a WebSocket-based version of streaming: a streaming-feel response UI where token chunks are queued per socket event and drained on a timer, progressively rendering text and markdown tables for a smooth experience from bursty transport.
highlights: Chunk-queue pattern smooths bursty WebSocket events into fluid rendering
Progressive markdown rendering mid-stream, including tables

projects.name: AI auto-replies for hosts
projects.description: AI-generated reply suggestions that meaningfully reduced host workload with no negative booking impact.

metrics.label: reduction in inquiries needing a host response

projects.name: Unified inbox redesign
projects.description: Led the web workstream of Airbnb's unified inbox redesign.

metrics.label: growth in web daily active users
users needing to file support tickets

projects.name: Inbox performance
projects.description: Directly responsible individual for inbox performance across multiple years: introduced inbox pagination and below-the-fold deferral of over-fetching UI. Maintained high test coverage.

aiTrustPatterns.summary: Distinguishes 'no data' (the question was understood but the answer isn't known) from 'no understanding' (the intent couldn't be parsed) so AI products fail honestly instead of guessing. Endorsed internally at Airbnb.
aiTrustPatterns.demonstratedBy: This very bot classifies every response as answered, no_data, or no_understanding.

leadership: Advised another major company's engineering leadership on server-driven UI architecture
Member of Airbnb's Web Tech Stack Working Group
Interim EM twice for a team of 8 while delivering as an IC

education.degree: BS Computer Science, minor in Cultural Anthropology

horrorMovies.title: Obsession
horrorMovies.summary: Baron 'Bear' Bailey breaks a novelty charm to force his co-worker Nikki Freeman to love him, but the supernatural compulsion warps her mind into violent obsession, trapping him in a nightmare he cannot wish away.
horrorMovies.take: Creepiest movie I've seen in years. Starts tense and doesn't let up. Highly recommend.

horrorMovies.title: Oddity
horrorMovies.summary: A psychic medium attempts to uncover the truth behind her sister's murder at the site of the crime.
horrorMovies.take: Constant dread and impressive use of one setting for the whole movie. It has a slow start but then gets really scary.

horrorMovies.title: Exhuma
horrorMovies.summary: The process of excavating an ominous grave unleashes dreadful consequences buried underneath.
horrorMovies.take: A winding tale that includes a ghost removal business and an awesome Japanese fox ghost.

horrorMovies.title: The Cabin in the Woods
horrorMovies.summary: Five teens visit a remote cabin and explore its cellar, finding strange items. When Dana reads from a book there, she unleashes zombie killers. But the situation is more complex than it appears.
horrorMovies.take: I first saw this in college after taking a Horror Film class and it became a favorite after first watch. It combines all your favorite tropes.

horrorMovies.title: Ready or Not
horrorMovies.summary: A bride's wedding night takes a sinister turn when her eccentric new in-laws force her to take part in a terrifying game.
horrorMovies.take: Just a good time. Funny and gross.

restaurants.name: Aziza
restaurants.note: The best corn bread and lamb you could ask for. Oh, and get the cous-cous.

restaurants.name: Thanh Long
restaurants.note: The OG garlic noodle place in SF. It's expensive but worth it.

restaurants.name: Lily
restaurants.note: Their beef salad is a must have.

restaurants.name: Shanghai Dumpling King
restaurants.note: My favorite dumpling spot. Decent Zhajiangmian.

restaurants.name: El Farolito
restaurants.note: Their burritos are the size of a new born baby — what's not to like?

restaurants.name: Lavash
restaurants.note: My old manager (shout out to Mojtaba) always said this was the best Persian restaurant in SF, and I heavily agree with that statement.

restaurants.name: Arsicault Bakery
restaurants.note: Get in line early and admire a bunch of really expensive road bikes while you wait to get pastries.

restaurants.name: Hook Fish Co
restaurants.note: Can't go wrong with the catch of the day.

articles.title: What is Generative UI? Building Agent-Powered Interfaces
articles.note: Generative UI is the industry converging on what Riley built at Airbnb: interfaces composed dynamically by AI from a card/component vocabulary rather than hardcoded screens — exactly the architecture of this bot.

articles.title: Errors + Graceful Failure
articles.note: The research-backed cousin of Riley's 'no data' vs 'no understanding' trust taxonomy: how AI products should fail honestly and give users a path forward.

fun: Girls Who Code mentor and hackathon coach
Avid knitter: a few baby sweaters, many beanies, and many half-constructed adult sweaters
Deep love of horror films, good and bad — watches pretty much every horror film that comes out
